review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to boundary protection. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but there is room for improvement in clarity and consistency. The functions accurately reflect the intent of SC-7-c by focusing on ingress and egress controls, as well as securing external connections.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_service_type_loadbalancer_restricted' to 'k8s_service_loadbalancer_usage_restricted' for better clarity and alignment with naming conventions.
title: Boundary Protection - Connect to external networks or...
step1_reasoning: The control SC-7-c focuses on boundary protection and preventing unauthorized access to external networks. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to network security controls that restrict ingress and egress traffic, as well as ensuring that services exposed to the internet are properly secured. NetworkPolicies can be used to enforce default deny rules for both ingress and egress traffic, effectively controlling what external networks can be accessed. Additionally, restricting the use of LoadBalancer service types and ensuring TLS is enabled for Ingress resources helps secure external connections.
